arguments.task: Open official Asana pages and extract evidence for: one-line product/API description, all documented authentication methods for the API/integrations, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, pricing or access gating relevant to developer API access, and whether Asana documents an MCP server. Prefer canonical first-party pages on developers.asana.com or official Asana pages. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted findings with quotes or paraphrases tied to each page.

input.thought: Start an official-source browser run to gather first-party evidence for Asana research fields.

raw_result.description: Work management platform with developer APIs for integrating with and extending the Asana Work Graph.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official authentication docs describe OAuth 2.0 for apps, Personal Access Tokens (PATs), Service Account tokens, and OpenID Connect for login. For this schema, PATs and service-account long-lived tokens map to token; OpenID Connect is not counted as an API auth method here because it is documented for user login rather than API authorization.
raw_result.gating_reason: Asana provides developer API documentation publicly, and the pricing page indicates free/personal plans include integrations and basic usage; some advanced developer/admin capabilities such as Service Accounts, Audit Log API, and SCIM are gated to Enterprise tiers.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API with reference coverage across many Asana objects and features, including tasks, projects, portfolios, goals, teams, custom fields, webhooks, audit logs, and a separate App Components API reference.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Asana has an official MCP Server documentation page describing MCP access to the Asana Work Graph for AI assistants.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate limits page states 150 requests/minute for free domains, 1,500 requests/minute for paid domains, 60 requests/minute for search endpoints, plus concurrent request limits (50 concurrent GET and 15 concurrent write requests) and additional cost-based limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official OpenAPI/REST reference, explicit rate-limit documentation, multiple documented auth options, and an official MCP Server page are strong signals for agent toolkit buildability. Some enterprise-only features exist, but core API access appears publicly documented and self-serve.
